title: WBJEE JELET 2026 Exam Date (June 13) - Full Schedule, Registration, Syllabus

detail.title: WBJEE JELET 2026 Exam Date (June 13) - Full Schedule, Registration, Syllabus
detail.short_info: WBJEE JELET 2026 exam date is June 13, 2026. Check full schedule, application from March 19, eligibility, syllabus, how to download admit card, and official links at wbjeeb.nic.in. Prepare effectively for lateral entry B.Tech/B.Pharm admission.

sections.heading: WBJEE JELET 2026 Exam Date (June 13) - Full Schedule, Registration, Syllabus

content.text: WBJEE JELET 2026 exam date is June 13, 2026. This pen-and-paper test by West Bengal Joint Entrance Examinations Board (WBJEEB) allows lateral entry to the second year of B.Tech/B.Pharm courses in West Bengal. Registration opens online on March 19, 2026.
Key dates and events are outlined below for quick reference.
The exam targets diploma or B.Sc. holders seeking direct second-year admission.

sections.heading: How to Download WBJEE JELET 2026 Admit Card

content.text: Admit cards become available in June 2026 on the official portal. Download using your application number and date of birth or password.

items: Visit wbjeeb.nic.in/jelet.​
Select the "Download Admit Card" option.
Enter your application number, DOB, and password.
Review details on screen and download the PDF.
Take a printout on A4 paper; bring it with photo ID to the exam center.​

content.text: It lists your photo, signature, exam venue, reporting time, and rules like no electronics allowed. Verify all info immediately—no edits after download.​
Applications are online-only via the WBJEEB portal starting March 19.​

items: Head to wbjeeb.nic.in/jelet and click "Apply Online."
Complete new registration with email and mobile for credentials.
Log in and enter personal, academic details (e.g., diploma percentage).
Upload passport photo and signature (JPG, 20-50KB).
Pay the fee online (card/net banking).
Submit, download confirmation, and keep it safe.

content.text: General fee is ₹500; reserved categories pay ₹400. Corrections open briefly in late March for select fields.​
Candidates must meet these basics to apply.

items: 3-year diploma in Engineering/Technology with 60% aggregate (45% for reserved).​
B.Sc. degree holders eligible for specific B.Tech branches.
Minimum age 17 years; no upper limit specified.
Indian nationals; West Bengal domicile not mandatory.

content.text: Check full norms in the official notification PDF.​
It's a 1-hour OMR sheet with 100 MCQs (1 mark each, no negative marking confirmed).​

rows: Math, Physics, branch-specific topics from diploma syllabus ​

content.text: Syllabus matches your qualifying diploma/B.Sc. level—download detailed PDF from site.​
Start with official syllabus and past papers for targeted prep.​

items: Build a 3-month plan: 4 hours daily on core subjects.
Practice 50-100 MCQs per topic; time yourself.
Take 2 full mocks weekly from April onward.
Revise formulas, diagrams daily; focus on speed.
Join online forums for doubts; stay healthy.​
Official Website: Click Here​
JELET Portal: Click Here​
Notification: Available on homepage post-March.
Helpline: Email/phone listed on site; check for updates.

content.text: Dates are tentative—confirm on official site to avoid surprises.
